title: Blend In A Lap-Grouped Ranking Expert Instead Of Another Pure Classifier
summary: ROC AUC rewards correct ordering, and pit calls are relative within a race-lap context: only a few drivers are plausible next-lap stoppers at once.
rationale: ROC AUC rewards correct ordering, and pit calls are relative within a race-lap context: only a few drivers are plausible next-lap stoppers at once. CatBoost and XGBoost both support pairwise/ranking objectives that can learn 'who is most likely to box now' rather than only absolute probabilities.
implementation_hint: Train a second expert with groups such as Year-Race-LapNumber using CatBoost PairLogit/YetiRankPairwise or XGBoost rank:pairwise. Use the same causal features, predict ranking scores for all rows, then OOF rank-normalize and blend with the current classifier rather than calibrating probabilities. Consider filtering or downweighting all-negative groups during training.
expected_effect: Can add complementary signal on within-lap ordering and improve AUC even if probability calibration does not move the metric.
risk: Many groups may have zero positives, making ranking training sparse or unstable; grouping choice and sample weighting matter.
